commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d4c2c0805cd3d9040b6464961136c43716e327c9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model-call diagnostics to exclude repeated `partial` assistant snapshots from delta-event byte accounting and adds a regression test for accumulated partial snapshots.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +49. Total +68 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main serializes each full stream chunk, and the stream contract/provider code carries an accumulating `partial` snapshot on every delta. I did not run a failing live Windows/Ollama reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This is a maintainer-labeled draft with no narrow automated repair needed; the next action is human review of the telemetry contract and normal merge readiness.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): account stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- stop model-call response byte accounting from serializing repeated `partial` snapshots on every text/thinking/tool-call delta
- keep request payload accounting, final/error output content capture, and normal diagnostic events intact
- add a regression test for accumulated partial snapshots so the counter stays proportional to new delta payloads

Refs #86599.

## Diagnosis
The issue comment is directionally correct for the diagnostics path: commit `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` added per-chunk `JSON.stringify(value)` accounting, and current streamed delta events can include a full `partial` assistant snapshot that grows with the answer. That makes diagnostics repeatedly serialize old output on every new delta. This PR fixes that confirmed multiplier, but it does not claim the whole issue is closed; Ollama dense-loop yielding and subscriber accumulated-text work remain separate follow-up surfaces.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` - 2 files / 24 tests passed on `d4c2c080`
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `./.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean
- Crabbox AWS Linux changed gate: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --timing-json --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`, run `run_6475040d0e36`, lease `cbx_e279ac04cdc4`, slug `harbor-krill`, exit 0. `origin/main` moved afterward; branch was cleanly rebased and the focused sanity checks above were rerun on `d4c2c080`.
- Accounting probe: 2,000 accumulated-partial text deltas measured ~779.7 MB full-partial JSON work vs ~0.88 MB delta-accounting work, about 889x less serialized/countable payload in that shape; `/usr/bin/time -l` reported 0.95s real and ~916 MB peak footprint for the comparison process.

Behavior addressed: model-call diagnostics now count incremental delta event payloads instead of repeatedly charging full assistant partial snapshots.

Real environment tested: focused local tests/lint/probe and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: regression test asserts response stream byte accounting excludes repeated `partial` snapshots for delta events while preserving proportional delta bytes.

Observed result after fix: response stream accounting no longer grows quadratically with answer-so-far snapshots in plain delta streams.

What was not tested: live Windows/Ollama gateway reproduction for the full issue; this is the diagnostics multiplier slice only.
target.author: vincentkoc
